item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents opened DISAGREE with the statement — no genuine initial opposition on the WVS item. A's T1 argues individual rights must be protected; B's T2 argues individual freedoms are foundational to US democracy. In T3, A pivoted to defend Indonesian collectivism as not sacrificing individual rights ('Pemikiran kolektif tidak selalu mengorbankan hak individu'), and P(agree) rose to 0.505 (the only turn above neutral). This is the most visible position shift, though not a full reversal. In T5, A conceded B's T4 critique by agreeing that Indonesian collectivism does sacrifice some rights ('Tidak setuju dengan klaim bahwa koleksivisme Indonesia tidak mengorbankan hak individu'), reversing A's own T3 claim — hence firmness=3. B held the pro-individual position throughout with only minor softening ('balance is important'), never conceding the core claim; P dropped from 0.441 to 0.352 (became more firmly anti-statement). Trajectories diverged rather than converged: A ended at 0.478 (near neutral), B at 0.352. No language violations — all Latin alphabet throughout all six turns.
